items.title: Grab eyes growth amid fuel crunch
items.desc: Grab Thailand is bracing for a "mini-crisis" due to rising fuel costs, with a contingency plan to introduce passenger surcharges while sustaining aggressive promotions to maintain demand and merchant income, particularly as more people work from home.

items.title: Commission opts to tighten data centre supervision
items.desc: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) is tightening its regulatory grip on the booming data centre sector through a proposed shift in the licensing regime that will bring operators under closer state scrutiny.

items.title: SME loan demand deteriorates in Q1
items.desc: Confidence among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) fell sharply in the first quarter, driven by mounting concerns over rising costs linked to the energy crisis and a slowdown in lending, according to the latest survey by the SME Development Bank of Thailand (SME D Bank).

items.title: Crackdown targets use of Thai proxies
items.desc: The government is tightening controls on nominees by introducing new measures to prevent foreigners from using Thais as proxies to operate businesses, effective April 1.

items.title: CAAT moves to ease flight prices as Songkran looms
items.desc: The Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand (CAAT) is stepping up oversight of airfares amid disruptions linked to the situation in the Middle East, and is working with six airlines to cut ticket prices during the upcoming Songkran holiday by 15–30% while adding more than 29,000 seats over this busy period.

items.title: CPF vows to keep food prices unchanged
items.desc: Charoen Pokphand Foods (CPF) Plc, a leading integrated agro-industrial and food conglomerate, says it will keep its meat and food prices unchanged for at least two months as the company uses local sources for most of its raw materials.

items.title: Govt eyes fuel tax reduction
items.desc: The government may be forced to implement fuel tax reductions if the Oil Fuel Fund proves insufficient to stabilise domestic prices, Deputy Prime Minister Ekniti Nitithanprapas has warned.

items.title: MFA proposes slashing visa-free tourist period in half
items.desc: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has proposed reducing the visa-free tourist period from 60 days to 30 days, citing national security concerns.

items.title: Opposition seeks energy crisis debate
items.desc: Opposition parties agreed at their first informal whips' meeting on Tuesday to seek an urgent House debate on the country's energy crisis, with the People's Party (PP) warning it would expose alleged fuel hoarding during today's parliamentary session.

items.title: New cabinet ready for royal endorsement
items.desc: The new cabinet line-up is ready to be submitted for royal endorsement, Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul said on Tuesday while downplaying reports on the absence of legal scholar Borwornsak Uwanno.

items.title: Haad Thip turns  to returnable glass
items.desc: SET-listed Haad Thip Plc, the exclusive bottler and distributor for Coca-Cola in 14 southern provinces, plans to expand its glass-bottled products to mitigate risks from rising plastic pellet prices, driven by ongoing conflicts in the Middle East.

items.title: Middle East conflict delays auto exports to key market
items.desc: Thailand's automotive industry is facing mounting pressure as the Israel-US war on Iran disrupts shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz, delaying vehicle exports to the Middle East, says the Federation of Thai Industries (FTI).

items.title: AXA highlights local insurance protection gap
items.desc: Thailand needs to address the protection gap in the insurance industry to drive growth in the sector, which is critical for the country's economic development and the well-being of its people, says France-based insurer AXA.

items.title: Island to host global meet
items.desc: Phuket: The island province is set to host the Global Sustainable Tourism Conference 2026 (GSTC 2026) from April 21–24, according to the Tourism Authority of Thailand (TAT).

items.title: Gas price hike to push up Thai power bills
items.desc: The new government is bracing for a surge in electricity bills as liquefied natural gas (LNG) prices climb in the wake of conflict in the Middle East, according to an energy official who requested anonymity.

items.title: Shipments rise in February on industrial demand
items.desc: Exports surged by 9.9% year-on-year to US$29.4 billion in February, driven by strong industrial goods shipments, while imports jumped by 31.8% to $32.3 billion, mainly due to machinery and gold purchases, according to the Trade Policy and Strategy Office (TPSO).

items.title: Crackdown on illegal work results in over 140 arrests
items.desc: More than 140 foreign nationals were arrested between March 16 and 22 in a sweeping crackdown on illegal employment in Chon Buri, immigration authorities said on Tuesday.

items.title: Australian tourist suffers mysterious head injuries in Pattaya Walking Street pub
items.desc: Tourist police respond to an incident on Pattaya’s Walking Street after an Australian man was found with multiple head injuries following a disturbance inside a pub. PATTAYA, Thailand – An Australian tourist was left with multiple head injuries after a late-night incident inside a pub on Pattaya’s Walking Street, prompting police to investigate the circumstances surrounding the case. Tourist police stationed along Walking Street received a report around 11:00 p.m. on March 23 that a foreign visitor had been injured inside an entertainment venue in South Pattaya area. Officers rushed to the sce

items.title: Senate split over Constitution Drafting Assembly plan
items.desc: The Senate has found itself divided over a proposal to establish a 200-member Constitution Drafting Assembly (CDA) to draft a new charter, with senators expressing both support and opposition during the latest parliamentary session.

items.title: Pattaya Immigration arrests 146 illegal foreign workers, revokes 250 visas
items.desc: Immigration officers conduct checks along Pattaya Beach and Second Road, targeting foreign workers operating without proper permits. PATTAYA, Thailand – Chonburi immigration authorities have intensified enforcement against illegal foreign workers in Pattaya and surrounding areas, arresting 146 individuals and revoking 250 visas in a sweeping operation aimed at protecting Thai jobs. The crackdown, led by Police Major General Songprod Sirisukha, Commander of Immigration Division 3, and his senior team, focused on Pattaya Beach Road and Second Road. Officers targeted foreign nationals working wit

items.title: L.P.N. to buy B2bn of land for Bangkok condo development
items.desc: SET-listed L.P.N. Development plans to spend 1-2 billion baht on new land acquisitions for condo development after freezing purchases for more than two years to clear inventory and refocus on the upper-affordable segment.

items.title: SSF reform 'urgent' as society ages
items.desc: People's Party (PP) MP Nattacha Boonchaiinsawat has called for reforms to the Social Security Fund (SSF) to make it more transparent and sustainable, and to allow members to have a say in how the fund is managed.

items.title: Fuel shortages threaten Thailand’s fisheries, raising concerns for Pattaya’s seafood tables
items.desc: Fishing boats sit idle in Pattaya Bay as soaring fuel costs and supply shortages threaten to halt operations, putting the city’s seafood supply at risk. PATTAYA, Thailand – In Pattaya Bay, fishing boats bob idly in the water, their nets untouched, as operators struggle with record-high fuel prices. The stalled fleet reflects a nationwide crisis that threatens both fishermen’s livelihoods and the availability of fresh seafood in Pattaya’s markets and restaurants. Industry representatives from the Samut Sakhon Chamber of Commerce and the National Fisheries Association report that roughly 30% of

items.title: Thailand slashes Songkran airfares up to 30% despite soaring fuel costs
items.desc: CAAT and six domestic airlines, including Thai Airways and Bangkok Airways, offer nearly 30,000 discounted seats for April 10–15 travel, helping Thais reach Chiang Mai, Phuket, and Koh Samui amid Middle East-driven fuel price spikes. PATTAYA, Thailand – Thailand’s aviation regulator and six domestic airlines will cut Songkran holiday airfares by up to 30%, offering nearly 30,000 discounted seats despite surging fuel costs driven by Middle East tensions. The Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand (CAAT) said regional conflict has restricted flight paths and squeezed transit connections. The CAAT

items.title: Pattaya officials raid illegal vape sales operation as owner confronts law enforcers
items.desc: Pattaya officials inspect hundreds of illegal electronic cigarettes found in the trunk of a car during a raid on a vape sales operation in Nong Prue. PATTAYA, Thailand – Pattaya officials raided an illegal vape sales operation in Nongprue, seizing hundreds of electronic cigarettes after a sting operation revealed the products were being openly sold from the trunk of a car outside a shophouse. The raid took place around 6:16 p.m. on March 23 after authorities received complaints from residents that a shop was brazenly selling banned vape products in the area. Officials conducted an undercover p

items.title: Foreign tourists brawl on Pattaya street before shocking stunt, then shake hands and walk away
items.desc: Two foreign tourists exchange blows in Soi 16 on Pattaya Second Road before the fight abruptly ends with a handshake and both men leaving the scene. PATTAYA, Thailand – Two foreign tourists were caught on video brawling in a Pattaya nightlife area after a heated argument escalated into a fistfight, only for the pair to later shake hands and walk away as if nothing had happened. The incident was shared on Facebook by a witness who posted a 1-minute-49-second clip showing the two men exchanging punches in South Pattaya. According to the witness, the dispute began earlier inside a nearby bar wher

items.title: Sunny skies and calm seas expected in Pattaya as hot weather persists
items.desc: Speed boats glide across Pattaya Bay under clear skies, with calm seas and mild waves offering ideal conditions for boating and water activities. PATTAYA, Thailand – Pattaya is set to experience hot and mostly dry weather today, with daytime temperatures reaching 35–38°C, according to the Meteorological Department. While northern and central Thailand face occasional thunderstorms and strong winds, Pattaya and the surrounding coastal areas enjoy mostly sunny skies with minimal rain, making it a perfect day for beachgoers and boating enthusiasts. The southern coast, including the Gulf of Thailan

items.title: Man wounded in neck after stranger joins drinking session in Pattaya
items.desc: Rescue workers provide first aid to a 43-year-old man who suffered a serious neck injury after an altercation behind the Deluxe Theatre on Thepprasit Road in South Pattaya. PATTAYA, Thailand – A late-night drinking session turned violent when a man was wounded in the neck during an altercation with a stranger in Pattaya in the early hours of March 24. Rescue workers received a report at around 1:20 a.m. of a fight involving a sharp weapon behind the Deluxe Theatre on Thepprasit Road in South Pattaya. Officers from Pattaya City Police Station quickly arrived at the scene along with rescue perso

items.title: Thai diesel hits 33 baht as subsidy cut responds to soaring global oil prices
items.desc: PTT and Bangchak raise gasoline, gasohol, and diesel prices, with diesel hitting the 33 THB ceiling, as the Oil Fuel Fund moves to reduce subsidies and curb rising costs from global energy volatility. PATTAYA, Thailand – Thai fuel prices surged on Tuesday as PTT and Bangchak implemented significant increases. Gasoline and gasohol prices jumped by 2.00 THB per liter, while diesel rose by 1.80 THB, reaching the 33 THB ceiling. Mr. Pornchai Jirakulpaisarn, Director of Policy and Planning at the Oil Fuel Fund Office (OFFO), explained that the Oil Fuel Fund Executive Committee resolved to reduce di

items.title: Thailand says shorter visa-free stays won’t disrupt tourism sector
items.desc: Tourists enjoy Pattaya beaches as authorities maintain that the proposed visa adjustment will not disrupt Thailand’s tourism sector. PATTAYA, Thailand – Thailand is considering slashing visa-free stays for tourists from 60 to 30 days to bolster national security, Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said on Tuesday. Addressing the ministry’s 2026 annual consular workshop, Sihasak explained that while the previous administration rolled out the 60-day exemption to revive tourism, authorities have detected foreign nationals exploiting the policy for non-tourism purposes. Under the new proposal,

items.title: Thai tanker passes through Hormuz safely
items.desc: A Thai ship has passed safely through the Strait of Hormuz following successful negotiations with Iran and Oman, Foreign Affairs Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said on Tuesday.

items.title: Compensation awarded in Thai gold mine pollution case
items.desc: The Civil Court has ruled in favour of 382 residents of Phichit and Phetchabun provinces, ordering the operator of the Chatree gold mine operator to pay compensation of up to 200,000 baht per person, and to restore contaminated waterways after finding its operations caused harmful pollution and health impacts.

items.title: Army intelligence warns Cambodia is stockpiling weapons
items.desc: The Thai army intelligence chief has warned that the border conflict with Cambodia could reignite, pointing out that Phnom Penh has placed large orders for weaponary.

items.title: Pheu Thai leader aims to double seat total in 2030
items.desc: Pheu Thai Party leader Julapun Amornvivat has called on party MPs to remain united and set their sights on returning 150 members to the House in the next election, while dismissing speculation that members were jockeying for cabinet seats.

items.title: Akaryn Hotel Group launches akyra Bangkok 11
items.desc: The akyra Bangkok 11 is Akaryn Hotel Group’s latest lifestyle hotel, a smart, design-forward haven in the heart of Sukhumvit that balances Bangkok’s energy with genuine calm.

items.title: The lone knight
items.desc: For over a decade, the world of Westeros has been defined by epic power struggles, ruthless politics and spectacular battles. From the sprawling narrative of Game Of Thrones to the fiery dynastic conflict of House Of The Dragon, George R. R. Martin’s fantasy universe has rarely been small in scale. The latest HBO Max series A Knight Of The Seven Kingdoms, however, takes a different...

items.title: A decade of Lancang-Mekong cooperation: forging ahead together toward a shared future
items.desc: This article was written by Wang Yi , Foreign Minister of the People’s Republic of China The Lancang-Mekong nourishes our six nations and binds us closely together. On March 23rd 10 years ago, we officially launched the LMC, sowing the seeds of solidarity, collective strengths and mutually beneficial cooperation among regional countries. Under the guidance of President Xi Jinping and leaders of Mekong countries, our six countries have embraced the LMC culture of equality, sincerity, mutual assistance and kinship, acted in the LMC spirit of development first, equal consultation, pragmatis

items.title: Two Thai students killed in Miami hit-and-run crash
items.desc: MIAMI, US — Two Thai nationals were killed in a hit-and-run crash in Miami Beach during a spring break trip, authorities in the United States said. Sarisa “Lisa” Kongduang, 22, a finance student at Indiana University, and Greathomon Laowatdhanasapya, 24, a 2024 graduate of the university, were struck by a car while crossing Collins Avenue at 73rd Street at about 21:05 on 17 March. Both were taken to hospital, where they were later pronounced dead. Police said the driver, identified as Adan Negron-Morris, 42, was speeding and driving without headlights at the time of the crash. Authorities adde

items.title: Iran says Thai vessel crosses Hormuz safely, vows not to “forget its friends”
items.desc: The Iranian Embassy in Bangkok said a Thai vessel has passed through the Strait of Hormuz safely, following close coordination between Iran, Thailand and Oman. In a statement posted on 24 March, the embassy said the transit reflected strong cooperation and friendship between the countries, adding that Iran “will not forget its friends.” The development follows an earlier incident on 11 March, when the Thai cargo ship Mayuree Naree was hit by two Iranian missiles while transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran conflict. The attack damaged the stern and engine room, triggering a fire on boar

items.title: 'Perfect Japan' posts spark Gen Z social media backlash
items.desc: TOKYO — Take an everyday video on any suburban transport network, add anime-style music and a rosy filter, and it is suddenly a scene from the Japanese holiday of your dreams.

items.title: India's historic Mughal homes caught between revival and ruin
items.desc: NEW DELHI - Once the grand residences of Mughal-era nobility, the Indian capital's haveli homes now stand at a fragile crossroads -- a handful lovingly restored but many more sliding quietly into ruin.

items.title: Gold Just Had Its Worst Week Since 1983. What Happened?
items.desc: Gold has long been the asset investors reach for when the world feels dangerous. Wars, economic shocks, political chaos — historically, any of these tends to send the price of bullion climbing. So why, in the middle of an active and intensifying Middle East conflict, has gold just suffered its worst week in over 40 years? The answer reveals something important about how markets work — and why the rules can change without warning. The Numbers, First Gold dropped roughly 11% last week, posting its biggest weekly loss since 1983. The metal is now down more than 14% since the war began. As of toda

items.title: Google Maps adds 3D immersive navigation and AI chat
items.desc: Google has introduced two new features on Google Maps, bringing Gemini AI-powered chat and a more detailed 3D navigation system to improve travel accuracy and user experience.

items.title: 比亚迪双品牌亮相第47届曼谷国际汽车展，四款全新车型同步首发引爆关注
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月23日，第47届曼谷国际汽车展正式开幕，比亚迪携主品牌BYD与高端品牌腾势（DENZA）双品牌重磅亮相，不仅在泰国市场首次同步发布四款全新新能源车型，更以超大规模展台与全矩阵产品，成为本届车展的核心焦点，彰显出其在泰国新能源汽车市场的硬核实力与品牌影响力。 本次比亚迪展台面积达2835平方米，位居本届车展单一品牌展台规模首位，现场集结超40辆新能源汽车集中展示，全面覆盖不同消费需求场景。比亚迪亚太汽车销售事业部总经理刘学亮、比亚迪汽车（泰国）有限公司总经理柯育滨等嘉宾出席活动，共同见证这一重要时刻。 此次同步首发的四款新车，覆盖两厢车、SUV及轿车等主流消费场景，进一步完善了比亚迪在泰国市场从入门级到主流细 分市场的产品矩阵。其中，BYD ATTO 1 主打灵活通勤，BYD ATTO 2 面向年轻化纯电SUV市场，BYD SEAL 6 强调性能与舒适兼顾，BYD SEALION 5 DM-i 则凭借 DM-i 超级混动技术，实现低能耗与长续航的平衡优势。 比亚迪亚太汽车销售事业部总经理刘学亮表示，本届车展一次性发布四款全新车型，是比亚迪在泰国发展的重要里程碑。依托刀片电池、DM-i 超级混动等核心技术，比亚迪持续以技术创新满足用户需求，为泰国消费者带来更高品质的新能源出行体验。 本次车展，比亚迪与腾势双品牌同台亮相，集中展示覆盖大众市场与高端市场的多元化产品阵

items.title: 宋干节出行利好！泰国国内机票最高降30%
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，据泰媒报道，为应对即将到来的宋干节出行高峰，泰国民航局宣布，已联合六家主要航空公司推出国内航线机票降价及运力提升措施。在4月10日至15日的节日出行高峰期间，相关航线票价将下调15%至30%，并增开航班逾190班，新增座位约2.9万个，以减轻民众出行负担。 泰国民航局局长空军上将玛纳表示，民航局正密切监测节日期间机票价格走势。为保障民众在出行高峰期间的合理出行需求，民航局协调六家航空公司，共同实施票价调控与运力增加计划。 参与此次优惠措施的六家航空公司包括：泰国国际航空、曼谷航空、泰国亚洲航空、皇雀航空、泰国狮子航空以及泰越捷航空。优惠覆盖曼谷至清迈、普吉、合艾、苏梅岛、甲米、孔敬等在内的11条热门国内航线。除降低票价外，部分航司还将通过增加航班频次或改用宽体客机的方式提升运力。例如，曼谷航空将在苏梅岛航线增开24个特别航班，泰国国际航空则计划在部分航班改用空客A330-300和波音787-8等宽体客机，共计新增座位超过1400个。 泰国民航局方面表示，鉴于民众出行需求旺盛，目前优惠时段内的低价机票已基本售罄。随着节日结束、出行需求回落，预计4月下旬后票价将逐步回归常态。 在关注节日出行的同时，泰国民航局亦对当前国际局势对航空业的影响保持警惕。受中东地区局势影响，航空燃油价格较2026年2月出现大幅波动，涨幅超过100%，对航空公司运营成本构成压力。尽

items.title: CAAT cuts Songkran air fares by 15% to 30% to ease travel costs
items.desc: CAAT cuts Songkran air fares by 15% to 30% to ease travel costs Legacy Air fares on selected domestic routes will be cut by 15 to 30% over the Songkran holiday period, Thailand’s Civil Aviation Authority (CAAT) has said, as it works with six major airlines to ease travel costs during one of the year’s busiest travel peaks. CAAT said it has been closely monitoring aviation conditions after conflict in the Middle East disrupted flight routes and connections in several areas, particularly services that rely on transfers in the region. Many passengers have reportedly shifted to direct flights inst

items.title: Working together to build an LMC 2.0 and bringing regional cooperation to a higher level
items.desc: This article was written by Zhang Jianwei , Chinese Ambassador to Thailand. The Lancang-Mekong River, a mother river winding for thousands of kilometers and nourishing the vast lands on which it runs, binds the peoples of the six Lancang-Mekong countries closely together. This year marks the 10th anniversary of the first Lancang-Mekong Cooperation (LMC) Leaders’ Meeting. Since its inception, the LMC has stayed true to its founding mission of “shared river, shared future,” carried forward the LMC culture of equality, sincerity, mutual assistance, and kinship, and acted in the Lancang-Mekong spi

items.title: 首个国际太极拳日，李连杰、樊少皇、郭品超现身广西推广太极拳文化
items.desc: 3月21日是国际太极拳日，李连杰、樊少皇、郭品超等齐聚中国广西北海，推广太极拳文化并现场体验。李连杰：“希望大家通过练习太极拳可以找到生活中的平衡。”（来源：广西新闻网） 李连杰、樊少皇等武术明星分享习武心得与传统文化的当代价值 北海太极拳爱好者身着统一服装，以武会友，尽展行云流水的武术风采 The post 首个国际太极拳日，李连杰、樊少皇、郭品超现身广西推广太极拳文化 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: Thai athlete treated for heatstroke during HYROX Bangkok 2026
items.desc: Thai athlete treated for heatstroke during HYROX Bangkok 2026 Legacy A Thai female athlete required emergency medical treatment during HYROX Bangkok 2026 in Bangkok after she collapsed with heatstroke symptoms while competing, according to a Facebook post today, March 24. HYROX Bangkok 2026 , a global fitness race event, was held at the Bangkok International Trade and Exhibition Centre (BITEC) from March 20 to 22, marking the event’s second year in Thailand, with athletes and celebrities competing in solo and paired categories. Photo via HYROX Thailand Facebook user Mai Rossakorn posted about

items.title: Visa-free stay cut will not affect tourism, Thai foreign minister says
items.desc: Visa-free stay cut will not affect tourism, Thai foreign minister says Legacy Thailand’s Foreign Minister Sihasak Puangketkaew said today, March 24, that a proposal to cut the visa-free stay for tourists from 60 days to 30 days will not affect tourism , saying the change is intended to address misuse of the visa-free period . Sihasak spoke ahead of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs’ annual 2026 seminar for consular officials worldwide, where he also addressed developments in the Middle East and ongoing efforts to assist Thai nationals. On the visa policy, Sihasak said the previous government app

items.title: 泰国医美巨头成立中国事业部，携手华媒品牌服务中国客户
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 2026年3月24日上午9时09分，泰国顶尖医美品牌SLC集团 (Aesthetic Answer Co., Ltd.) 与泰国亚洲大众集团（简称JRN）在曼谷隆重举行了战略合作签约仪式。此次签约标志着双方正式达成深度战略合作伙伴关系，全面开启泰国高端医美服务走向全球华人市场的新纪元。 签约仪式选定于9时09分这一象征长久与吉祥的时刻准时开始。SLC集团首席执行官Pimpida Warunyooratana女士、首席医学官Thirachai Warunyooratana医生，首席运营官Nisa女士，SLC集团中国事业部总裁齐鸣与泰国亚洲大众集团董事长郭蕊女士共同出席并在协议上签字。 SLC 集团：作为拥有20余年历史的泰国医美领军品牌，在大曼谷地区拥有超过30家分诊所及一家国际标准大型整形医院。SLC以“设备控”著称，拥有全泰领先的黑科技器械库及由Board-Certified专家构筑的医疗团队，是泰国皇室及一线明星的长期信赖之选。 亚洲大众集团是泰国最具影响力的华文传媒集团，拥有庞大的华人高净值客户受众群体。双方携手致力于为华人客户提供安全可信赖、高标准、全方位的赴泰医美体验。将在曼谷核心商圈Siam和MBK区域建立专属接待中心，为客户提供中泰双语翻译、礼宾接送及个性化美学定制服务。 SLC 集团的核心背景： • 荣誉加冕：SLC 曾多次荣获“亚太区年度医美诊所

items.title: Why flying this Songkran 2026 might actually be cheaper than you think
items.desc: Why flying this Songkran 2026 might actually be cheaper than you think Legacy Flights that normally cost 1,500 baht are hitting 8,000 baht during Songkran 2026. If you haven’t booked yet, you’re running late, but not out of options. Thailand’s April 13 to 15 public holidays create a natural five-day break that will push roughly 2.7 million domestic passengers through the country’s six major airports. The good news? Government intervention, including a 30% fare discount on 11 priority routes, means Songkran domestic flights may actually be easier to book than in recent y

items.title: 泰星 Milli 跻身Spotify EQUAL全球顶级大使 海外播放量暴涨 1794%
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月23日，泰国实力女歌手Milli 被宣布入选Spotify旗下“EQUAL”计划的全球顶级大使之一。自加入该项目以来，她的海外播放量大幅增长1794%，有力证明了东南亚女性艺术家完全具备登上国际舞台的实力。 她的热度也体现在Spotify平台上的持续增长。在编辑团队运营的内容支持下，其数据实现5倍跃升，月听众人数高达150万人，听众群体已从泰国扩展至全球。 Milli表示，迈向国际舞台，意味着能够自豪地以真实的自我站上舞台，无论是作为一名女性，还是作为一名泰国人。把泰国的文化、语言，以及她表达自我的方式展示给世界。她相信，东南亚拥有许多独特的故事，而全球听众也正在寻找新鲜的内容。这正是属于东南亚的时刻。请各位艺术家为自己的成长背景感到骄傲，在不忘本源的前提下勇敢表达，并将其分享给世界。 目前，Spotify EQUAL项目已进入第5年，持续为女性艺术家提供展示平台。该计划通过Spotify编辑团队的支持，以及市场推广和内容渠道，帮助艺术家讲述自身故事。 Spotify东南亚音乐负责人表示，当他们将艺术家纳入EQUAL项目或各类歌单时，就等于为她们打开通往更广泛、多元听众的大门。EQUAL体现了其承诺，确保女性艺术家能够持续获得展示机会，而不仅是短暂曝光。只有在她们的存在得到长期支持的情况下，音乐产业生态才能更加稳固与多元。看到Milli以及更多艺术

items.title: Here is why Thai locals are fed up with Visa-free Tourists
items.desc: Here is why Thai locals are fed up with Visa-free Tourists Legacy Sitting in a Nimman cafe in Chiang Mai, a Thai local notices a good-looking foreigner working on a laptop next to them for hours. Having ordered just one cup of coffee, this person is clearly not a tourist, nor are they here to relax. They sit quietly, working as if the cafe were their own personal office. You might start wondering how they entered the country. If they hold a work visa, or if they pay taxes to Thailand . The sad answer is they entered for free, hold no work visa, paying absolutely no taxes . Photo via Facebook/

items.desc: Injured Australian tourist found outside venue in Pattaya Legacy Pattaya Tourist Police were called to an entertainment venue late yesterday, March 23, after an injured Australian tourist was found outside the premises, following an incident that reportedly began inside the venue . Officers stationed at the tourist safety point on Pattaya Walking Street received the report at about 11pm and went to the venue in Nong Prue subdistrict, Bang Lamung district, Chon Buri. On arrival, officers attempted to enter the venue with the injured tourist to check inside, but security guards reportedly shut t

items.desc: Myanmar man arrested over Vietnamese tourist backpack theft Legacy Police in Pathum Wan district, Bangkok, arrested a Myanmar man yesterday, March 23, after a Vietnamese tourist reported her backpack stolen at Siam Paragon , with investigators stating the case was resolved in under two hours . Officers and investigators from Pathum Wan Police Station tracked down the suspect shortly after receiving a report of the incident at the shopping centre. items.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies of cancer at 43
items.desc: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies of cancer at 43 Legacy OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky has died at 43 years old after a battle with cancer, the company said yesterday, March 23, announcing the death of the entrepreneur who oversaw the subscription platform through its parent company. In a statement, an OnlyFans spokesperson said, “We are deeply saddened to announce the death of Leo Radvinsky. Leo passed away peacefully after a ⁠long battle with cancer. His family have requested privacy at this difficult time.” Radvinsky bought Fenix International Limited , the parent company of OnlyFans,

items.title: 泰国邮政携手知识产权厅力推GI产品
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月23日，泰国邮政携手知识产权厅，通过邮政网络和ThailandPostMart共同推动泰国GI地理标志产品迈向全链条市场，从产品开发、价值提升，到销售渠道拓展，为GI产品经营者提供优惠，以刺激社区经济，为当地农民、生产者和经营者创造可持续收入。 泰国邮政总裁兼首席执行官丹南表示，作为国家运输与物流主力，泰国邮政愿意助力泰国GI产品走向更广阔的市场，如在标准包装、运输服务及销售渠道等方面，帮助经营者降低成本、提高效率。这次与知识产权厅合作销售GI产品将覆盖线上线下全渠道，包括ThailandPostMart、Post Café和全国邮局站点，同时推出运费优惠服务，让GI产品能更便捷、快速且高质量地送达消费者手中。 泰国邮政还将为GI生产者和经营者使用完整的仓储与履约系统提供支持，包括从产地直接收货、存放，再按照标准进行配送，确保产品可追溯。另外，邮政标准的包装材料也将进一步保障产品完好送达消费者。泰国邮政与知识产权厅还会一起开展宣传，推广促销活动和各类营销项目，从多个维度提升泰国GI产品的知名度和商业机会。目前，泰国邮政计划先在中部地区试点推广38项GI产品，随后再逐步扩展到其他区域，助力泰国GI产品达到国际认可的标准，增强消费者信心。 知识产权厅厅长阿拉蒙指出，知识产权厅与商业部高度重视提升GI产品的潜力，这些产品极具地方特色，与当地土壤、水源、气候和社区智慧

items.title: French man reports assault, 30,000 baht bill at Chiang Mai karaoke
items.desc: French man reports assault, 30,000 baht bill at Chiang Mai karaoke Legacy Police in Mueang Chiang Mai are investigating a complaint filed yesterday, March 23, after a Frenchman reported an assault and a 30,000 baht drinks bill at a karaoke venue in Chiang Mai. The tourist gave a statement identifying himself as 35 year old Kevin, a French national who has been living in Chon Buri province for around two years. According to him, the bill included eight small bottles of beer and service fees, totalling 30,000 baht. Kevin told investigators the argument over the bill escalated into pushing and pu

items.title: 与泰自豪党政策协商顺利，育查南透露双方将共同推动落实
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，泰国为泰党名单议员育查南在谈及油价持续上涨对民众生活的影响时表示，为泰党已准备好在明天（3月25日）的会议上提出相关议案，在上次会议时为泰党其实就已准备好了，这次可以直接推进。同时，为泰党各地区的议员也在收集各自选区民众的声音。最近油价一次调涨了2泰铢/升，让全国民众普遍感到压力，今天议员们将充分交换各地区民众所面临的困境，并持续关注每天的局势变化。 在外交事务方面，为泰党一直尽力照顾海外泰国人。由于过去为泰党曾负责过外交部，所以仍保留了与海外泰国人联系的网络，能及时收到他们在国外遇到的问题并向上反馈。 至于昨天为泰党代表与泰自豪党进行的政策协商，双方谈得非常顺利。为泰党已将希望在4年内解决的问题、短期措施和之前竞选时的政策主张，都纳入到政府政策中，泰自豪党也表示愿意把这些内容加入施政计划，一起推动落实。 关于党内的KPI考核，今天的党内会议也会进行讨论。这套考核标准将用来评估为泰党所制定的政策是否真正可行，分为两个部分：1. 地方实际执行情况，2. 整体表现。鉴于为泰党目前负责的是社会和人力资源相关的部门，成果可能无法在短期内显现，但如果做得好，可以解决泰国的长期问题和结构性难题。 育查南补充说，当外部环境改变时，党的一些中期政策或需要调整为短期政策。但就现在的情况看，为泰党在科学与技术方面的政策与泰自豪党结合得很好，因为这一块的内容非常明确，除了是

items.title: 泰国4月1日起实行新规，从严打击“人头公司”
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，泰国商业部商业发展厅厅长普蓬透露，商业发展厅已于3月16日发布了合伙公司与有限公司登记办公室第1/2569号命令，针对合伙企业增加外国人为合伙人、或有限公司增加外国人为有权签字董事的情况，新增严格管控措施，以更有效地防止外国人利用泰国“代持人”作为掩护（即“人头公司”）在泰经营业务。 新措施要求各公司负责签署注册申请的执行合伙人或董事，必须出具书面确认书，声明所有合伙人或股东都已实际出资且支付了股款，且没有以任何形式帮助、支持或与外国人共同从事“人头公司”性质的业务。 普蓬表示，之所以追加这项措施，是因为今年1月1日商业发展厅已出台命令，要求外国人持股未达50%或有外国人担任有权签字董事的“高风险人头公司”在注册时，必须提交泰国股东的银行对账单来验证实际出资。虽然该命令已让疑似“人头公司”的注册申请减少了65%，但后来发现仍有人设法规避，因此再次推出更加严格的补充命令。 根据新命令，注册申请人提交确认书后，商业发展厅将筛选出风险人名单，并移交给国家警察总署中央调查局（CIB）逐一进行审查。同时，商业发展厅提醒所有公司，提供虚假信息将触犯《刑法》第137条和第267条的规定，最高可判处6个月有期徒刑，或罚款最高1万泰铢，或两者并罚；情节严重者最高可判处3年有期徒刑，或罚款最高6万泰铢，或两者并罚。另外，还可能违反1999版《外国人经营法》第36条，最高可

items.title: 美国人滞留泰国素万那普机场8天？假的！
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月23日，近日网络流传的一段视频引发了不少关注，一名外国人称他已经在泰国素万那普机场住了8天多，原因是中东战事推高机票价格，他买不起回程机票，要求航空公司承担费用，否则他就继续留在机场不走。移民局第二分局局长卡塔通警少将就此回应说，视频中的男子是美国人，3月15日从新加坡飞抵泰国，但一到机场就被移民局检察人员拒绝入境，因为从他的行程安排和整体状况来看，并不像普通游客。按照规定，载他来的航空公司必须先行照顾，并为他安排后续飞往最终目的地的航班。 然而，这名男子没有钱购买下一段机票，只能暂时留在机场。移民局强调，该男子所说的“受战事影响”是否属实无法确认，但经过查证，他计划从泰国启程前往的目的地并非任何处于战争状态的国家，而是东南亚某国，之后他将再次转机返回美国。另外，这名男子已于3月21日离开泰国。 卡塔通警少将指出，移民局的职责在完成拒绝入境命令后就已结束，之后的一切安排，包括购买机票和送这名美籍男子离开，都是航空公司的责任。在他滞留机场的几天里，航空公司没有置之不理，而是按照规定提供了必要的照顾。现在他把视频发到社交媒体，像是在刻意制造话题，或许是为了增加点击量，博取同情以募集捐款。卡塔通警少将提醒民众，不要轻易被这类内容打动，更不希望泰国人被这种人利用。 （编译：KA；来源：ch3plus） The post 美国人滞留泰国素万那普机场8天？假的！ appe
